title: astragalus subumbellatus
intro: Astragalus subumbellatus, also known as Mongolian milkvetch, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Mongolia and has been traditionally used in medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory, antioxidant, and anti-aging effects, leading to various health benefits such as reduced stress, improved immune function, and enhanced cognitive performance. Astragalus subumbellatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine and modern herbal supplements.
